creator: koitsu@FreeBSD.org
text:
I can't check on RELENG_7 because we don't have any machines running
that which use postgrey, but I can confirm that the problem continues to
exist on RELENG_6.  In fact, something strange happened recently
pertaining to this.

postgrey was restarted on 01/15 (Tuesday) after we built world/kernel on
our server and did maintenance.  The machine mounts /proc automatically
from /etc/fstab.  Sometime between 01/05 and now, the process title
for the postgrey daemon changed to '[perl5.8.8]':

$ ps -auxwwwwww | grep postgrey
postgrey 79212  0.0  0.3  8864  7976  ??  Ss   Tue01pm   0:18.68 [perl5.8.8]

I decided to unmount /proc and re-mount it to see if the problem went
away -- and it did:

$ su2
eos# umount /proc
eos# ps -auxwwwwww | grep postgrey
postgrey 79212  0.0  0.2  8864  7704  ??  Is   Tue01PM   0:18.68 [perl5.8.8]
eos# ls -l
eos# mount /proc
eos# ps -auxwwwwww | grep postgrey
postgrey 79212  0.0  0.2  8864  7704  ??  Is   Tue01PM   0:18.68 /usr/local/sbin/postgrey --pidfile=/var/run/postgrey.pid --inet=10023 -d --user=postgrey --group=postgrey --dbdir=/var/db/postgrey --delay=240 --hostname=mx01.sc1.parodius.com --whitelist-clients=/conf/ME/postfix/postgrey_whitelist_clients --whitelist-recipients=/conf/ME/postfix/postgrey_whitelist_recipients (perl5.8.8)

Now, I don't deny there's a good chance RELENG_7 addresses all of this
(in the sense that it *really* does not need /proc mounted), but I don't
have a way of confirming it at this time.  We have a new box being built
that will run RELENG_7, and all the data/users from the RELENG_6 box
will be migrated there, so after that's done I should be able to
confirm/deny whether the issue occurs on 7.
